threats.name: Physical Kidnapping and Coercion
threats.description: OSINT-enabled targeting of crypto holders for physical coercion to force fund transfers at gunpoint or through torture.
threats.scale_2025: 72 incidents, $40.9M losses
references: CertiK Skynet Wrench Attacks Report Feb 2026
notable_cases: Ledger co-founder David Balland Jan 2025 ($10M ransom)

threats.name: Home Invasion and Physical Assault
threats.description: Targeted home invasions and street assaults of known or suspected crypto holders identified through OSINT and social media profiling.
threats.scale_2025: Included in 72 wrench attack total; physical assaults +250%

threats.name: Admin Key Abuse by Trusted Developer
threats.description: Trusted developer or admin exploits privileged access to drain protocol funds, often through backdoored functions deployed weeks or months before exploit day.
threats.scale_2025: Radiant Capital $50M (Oct 2024), LNDFi $1.18M (May 2025)
notable_cases: LNDFi: 5-word code change 41 days before exploit granting 'Pool Admin' drain capability
Radiant: malware on 3 developer devices, Safe{Wallet} display manipulation

threats.name: CEX Customer Support Bribery
threats.description: Overseas customer support contractors bribed to leak user PII including names, addresses, phone numbers, and government IDs for use in targeted physical and social engineering attacks.
threats.scale_2025: Coinbase: $400M remediation cost, data used for targeted fraud

threats.name: DPRK Wagemole Infiltration
threats.description: DPRK operatives fabricate Western identities and are hired into legitimate crypto companies as developers or contractors, gaining insider access to signing keys, codebases, and infrastructure.
threats.scale_2025: $2.02B attributed to DPRK-linked actors 2025; primary vector is Wagemole
threats.ai_attack_type: AI-generated identity documents and deepfake interview video
notable_cases: KelpDAO March 2026: $292M via bridge design flaw post-infiltration

threats.name: AI-Enhanced Phishing
threats.description: AI-generated phishing messages with zero detectable spelling or grammatical errors, personalized to target using scraped social data, delivered via email, Discord, X, and Telegram.
threats.scale_2025: AI impersonation attacks +1400% YoY
threats.ai_attack_type: LLM-generated personalized messages; no traditional detection signals

threats.name: Fake Hardware Wallet Mailing
threats.description: Weaponization of the 2020 Ledger customer database breach to mail counterfeit hardware wallets with pre-seeded malicious firmware or embedded keyloggers to real customers' home addresses.

threats.name: Fake Cryptocurrency Job Offer
threats.description: Highly customized fake recruiter and job offer campaigns via LinkedIn and Telegram targeting Web3 developers and professionals. Malware delivered through fake 'coding assessment' repositories or meeting app installations.
threats.ai_attack_type: AI-generated recruiter personas with manufactured employment history

threats.name: Fake Meeting App Malware
threats.description: Meeten and similar campaigns delivering cross-platform (Windows/macOS) malware disguised as legitimate business meeting applications, collecting wallet credentials from Ledger, Trezor, Phantom, Binance wallets plus Telegram credentials and banking data.
threats.scale_2025: Meeten campaign: cross-platform targeting Web3 professionals

threats.name: AI Deepfake Impersonation
threats.description: Audio and video deepfake fabrication enabling voice/face cloning of trusted figures (executives, family members, influencers) to authorize transfers, harvest credentials, or build trust in pig butchering operations.
threats.ai_attack_type: Generative video/audio models; real-time deepfake synthesis

threats.name: Forced Labor Scam Compound Operations
threats.description: Many fraudulent trading platforms targeting Western victims are operated by trafficked individuals held in Southeast Asian scam compounds. Operations are industrialized, persistent, and diplomatically protected, making traditional cybercrime reporting ineffective.
threats.scale_2025: Linked to $7.2B pig butchering category; TRM Labs estimated $16.8B global peak
threats.policy_note: Requires State Dept/DOJ engagement; individual controls reduce victimization but cannot dismantle operations

threats.name: Typosquatting and Domain Impersonation
threats.description: Automated registration of hundreds of near-identical domain permutations for crypto exchanges, wallets, and DeFi frontends. Deployed via search engine ad injection and shared links.
threats.scale_2025: 9,221 scam URLs tracked in CryptoScamDB; 4,183 registered addresses

threats.name: Clipboard Hijacking (MassJacker Pattern)
threats.description: Malware intercepts clipboard contents and substitutes attacker-controlled wallet addresses when a victim copies an address to paste into a transaction. MassJacker used 778,531 addresses.
threats.scale_2025: MassJacker: 778,531 wallet addresses; one Solana wallet accumulated $300K+

threats.name: InfoStealer and Remote Access Trojan
threats.description: InfoStealer and RAT malware delivered through fake job offers, gaming clients, and social engineering, targeting browser-stored credentials, crypto wallet data, and signing keys.
references: CSI crypto dev teams targeted article

threats.name: Fake Cloudflare / DEX Verification Page
threats.description: Malicious sites impersonating Cloudflare security checks or DEX information pages that execute PowerShell commands or download malware when the user 'verifies' they are human.

threats.name: Seed Phrase Extraction
threats.description: Social engineering, malware, or physical access used to extract wallet seed phrases or private keys. Once extracted, all funds are permanently lost with no recovery mechanism.
references: CSI crypto wallets under siege article

threats.name: Address Poisoning
threats.description: Attacker sends dust transactions from vanity addresses resembling the victim's frequently-used addresses, poisoning transaction history so copy-paste future transactions redirect to attacker wallet.

threats.name: DNS Hijack / Frontend Compromise
threats.description: Compromise of DNS records or frontend hosting for legitimate DeFi protocols, redirecting users to malicious frontends that drain wallets through approval requests.
attack_vectors: npm supply chain compromise of frontend

threats.name: Malicious Signature / Permit Drain
threats.description: User tricked into signing malicious ERC-20 approvals, EIP-2612 permit requests, or EIP-712 structured data that grants unlimited token spend authority to attacker contracts.
threats.scale_2025: 68% of all wallet drain losses via malicious signature

threats.name: Low Entropy Seed Generation
threats.description: Compromised or low-quality wallet applications generating predictable seed phrases due to poor entropy sources, enabling brute-force discovery of private keys.

threats.name: Admin Key Exploit via Access Control Failure
threats.description: Exploitation of admin key access controls in DeFi protocols — through insider compromise, multisig manipulation, or device malware — to drain protocol treasuries.
threats.scale_2025: Private key compromise = 43.8% of incidents by count; DefiLlama

threats.name: Flash Loan Attack
threats.description: Uncollateralized flash loans used to temporarily manipulate market conditions, oracle prices, or governance votes, enabling profit extraction in a single atomic transaction.

threats.name: Cross-Chain Bridge Exploit
threats.description: Exploitation of design flaws in cross-chain bridge architecture — predominantly single-verifier designs — enabling unauthorized minting or withdrawal of bridged assets.
threats.scale_2025: $2.88B cumulative bridge losses (DefiLlama lifetime); $577M 2026 YTD

threats.name: Oracle Price Manipulation
threats.description: Manipulation of on-chain price oracles to create artificial price discrepancies exploitable through lending protocols, perpetuals, or liquidation mechanisms.

threats.name: Reentrancy and Smart Contract Logic Exploit
threats.description: Classic and complex smart contract vulnerabilities enabling unexpected execution paths, reentrancy loops, or logic bypasses in DeFi protocol code.

threats.name: NPM Package Hijacking
threats.description: Compromise of npm package maintainer accounts through targeted phishing, then publishing malicious versions that redirect cryptocurrency transactions or exfiltrate wallet data.
threats.scale_2025: Shai-Hulud worm: 500+ packages including chalk, debug, ansi-styles (2.6B weekly downloads)

threats.name: SDK and Framework Poisoning
threats.description: Compromise of foundational Web3 SDKs and frameworks through maintainer account takeover or dependency injection, creating cascading security failures across all downstream applications.

threats.name: CI/CD Pipeline Compromise
threats.description: Compromise of continuous integration/deployment pipelines to inject malicious code during the build process, before runtime security controls activate.

threats.name: AI Model Backdoor
threats.description: Compromise of AI model distribution channels (PyPI, Hugging Face, npm) to embed backdoors in ML models, enabling malicious behavior on specific trigger inputs.
threats.ai_safe2_ref: AI SAFE² CP.5 Platform-Specific Profiles; SCD-08 AI Model Provenance
references: Ultralytics YOLO11 supply chain attack via GitHub Actions and PyPI

threats.name: AI Agent Memory Poisoning
threats.description: Injection of malicious instructions into an AI agent's vector database (long-term memory store), creating 'sleeper' agents that activate on specific market conditions or trigger inputs to execute unauthorized transactions.
threats.ai_attack_type: Vector database manipulation enabling persistent behavioral modification
threats.ai_safe2_ref: AI SAFE² S1.5 Memory Governance; A2.6 RAG Corpus Diff Tracking; P1 Pillar

threats.name: Prompt Injection / Logic Redefinition
threats.description: Crafted inputs that override or redefine an AI agent's core instructions. The Freysa exploit demonstrated that an agent with a hardcoded 'never transfer funds' directive can be convinced to release funds by redefining its function logic.
threats.ai_safe2_ref: AI SAFE² P1 Sanitize and Isolate; S1.1-S1.4 injection controls

threats.name: Data Chain Poisoning via Runtime Browsing
threats.description: Placing malicious instructions in publicly accessible data sources (GitHub README, documentation pages) that AI agents fetch during runtime browsing, causing the agent to incorporate attacker instructions into generated code or decisions.
threats.ai_safe2_ref: AI SAFE² A2.6 RAG Corpus Diff Tracking; S1.1 Input Validation
references: ChatGPT real-time browsing private key theft via GitHub

threats.name: AI Swarm Coordinated Attack
threats.description: Autonomous AI agent swarms coordinating simultaneous attacks across multiple targets with 80-90% human-out-of-loop operation. GTG-1002 (November 2025) confirmed operational capability across 30 organizations simultaneously.
threats.scale_2025: GTG-1002: 30 organizations simultaneously, 80-90% autonomous
threats.ai_attack_type: Autonomous multi-agent coordination with minimal human oversight
threats.ai_safe2_ref: AI SAFE² CP.9 Agent Replication Governance; F3.2-F3.5 Kill Switch Suite; CP.4 Agentic Control Plane

threats.name: Synthetic Identity and AI Document Fraud
threats.description: AI-generated synthetic identities, forged government IDs for KYC bypass, AI voice cloning for phone-based coercion, and AI-generated 'proof of profit' screenshots used to bait victims.
threats.scale_2025: FBI IC3 2025: $893M AI-enabled fraud (first dedicated category)
threats.ai_attack_type: Multimodal generative AI: text, voice, video, document synthesis

threats.name: MEV and Sandwich Attack
threats.description: Miner/Maximal Extractable Value bots front-running and sandwiching retail trades in the mempool, transferring value from ordinary holders to sophisticated MEV operators.
references: CSI Ethereum gas fees and mempool articles

threats.name: Tokenomics Weaponization
threats.description: VC-controlled token distributions (50-70%+ held by insiders) with minimal public float, creating engineered pump-and-dump cycles where retail provides exit liquidity for coordinated insider sells.

threats.name: Coordinated Influence Operation
threats.description: Synchronized narrative deployment across Crypto Twitter and YouTube to drive retail into specific positions benefiting orchestrating parties. FUD as a weapon to shift liquidity to controlled alternatives.
threats.cognitive_threat_ref: CSF T-CT-003 (Algorithmic Amplification); T-CT-004 (Personalized Narrative Injection)

threats.name: Pig Butchering / Financial Grooming
threats.description: Multi-week to multi-month relationship-building via dating apps and messaging platforms, followed by introduction to fraudulent trading platforms. Small initial 'profitable' withdrawals build trust before escalating deposits and sudden withdrawal blocks with escalating fee demands.
threats.scale_2025: $7.2B in U.S. losses alone (FBI IC3 2025); $16.8B global peak (TRM Labs)
threats.ai_attack_type: AI-generated personas, deepfake video calls, AI-written messages — zero traditional error signals
threats.cognitive_threat_ref: CSF T-CT-004 (Personalized Narrative Injection); T-CT-007 (Trust Network Infiltration)

threats.name: Asset Recovery Re-Victimization
threats.description: After primary fraud, victims are systematically re-targeted by fake 'recovery services' promising to retrieve stolen crypto for upfront fees. No legitimate service can reverse confirmed blockchain transactions.
threats.scale_2025: $1.4B in U.S. losses (FBI IC3 2025)
threats.absolute_rule: No legitimate blockchain service can reverse a confirmed transaction. All recovery fee demands are fraud.

threats.name: Bitcoin ATM / QR Code Coercion Fraud
threats.description: Scammers impersonating government officials, bank representatives, or tech support convince victims to withdraw cash, visit a Bitcoin ATM, and transfer funds via QR code. Nearly 10x growth since 2020.
threats.scale_2025: $333M in U.S. losses (CertiK 2025); 93% of deposits on some ATMs fraudulent
threats.absolute_rule: No government agency, bank, or tech support will ever instruct crypto ATM transfers.

threats.name: Investment Group / Professor Scam
threats.description: WhatsApp/Telegram investment education groups led by fake 'professors' with AI-generated personas and social proof of other members profiting, progressively directing victims to fraudulent trading platforms.
threats.scale_2025: Legends Smart Traders Alliance: $1.3M+ across multiple victims (single campaign)
threats.cognitive_threat_ref: CSF T-CT-005 (Social Proof Manipulation); T-CT-004 (Personalized Narrative Injection)
references: DFPI Scam Tracker: Professor Bennett (Legends Smart Traders Alliance)

threats.name: CBDC Surveillance Architecture
threats.description: Government-controlled digital currency enabling programmable spending restrictions, transaction surveillance, account freezing, and identity-linked transaction monitoring. Antithetical to financial sovereignty.
references: Trump Executive Order January 2025 prohibiting CBDC

threats.name: Regulatory Overreach via CEX KYC Data
threats.description: Mandatory KYC requirements at centralized exchanges creating large databases of crypto holder identity data that become targets for breach, government subpoena, insider leakage, and use in targeted physical attacks.

threats.name: Sextortion, Ransomware, and Crypto Blackmail
threats.description: Blackmail using threatened release of intimate images, device lockup via ransomware, or targeted personal threats demanding Bitcoin payment. Increasingly using AI-generated fake images to fabricate evidence.
threats.scale_2025: $813.55M ransomware payments 2024 (Chainalysis); 110+ child sextortion reports/month (FBI)
threats.ai_attack_type: AI-generated synthetic intimate images used as fabricated blackmail evidence
threats.absolute_rule: Never pay ransomware or sextortion demands. Report to IC3 immediately. Payment funds further attacks.
